project: VA Loan Lead Funnel - Cornerstone First Mortgage
description: A VA loan lead generation website for Cornerstone First Mortgage. Features a quiz funnel, 20 SEO-optimized blog posts, veteran appreciation messaging, and direct-to-VA-guidelines underwriting. Theme: 'They served for us, now we serve them.'

technical_decisions: Quiz captures: state, credit score, loan amount, loan purpose, property type, property address interest, military branch, service status
Blog posts: 20 posts, 2000+ words each, stored in data/posts.json
Color scheme: Military-inspired - Navy (#0A1628), OD Green (#556B2F), Gold (#D4A84B), Cream (#F5F5DC)
No VA loan overlays - we underwrite direct to VA guidelines (no 620 FICO requirement)
In-house underwriting team specializing in VA loans
GEO optimization: Use /geo-content-optimizer and /seo-geo skills for AI citability
Design: Slick, modern, Google Ads ready with strong CTAs
tradeoffs: Blog posts written in authentic human voice - no AI-sounding content
Veteran appreciation focus over hard sell tactics
Trust-building content over aggressive conversion tactics
edge_cases: Low credit score veterans - we CAN help them unlike most lenders
Complex VA eligibility scenarios (surviving spouse, reservists, etc.)
VA funding fee exemptions for disabled veterans
constraints: Must be compliant with VA advertising guidelines
No false claims about rates or approval guarantees
All content must honor veterans appropriately

brand_messaging.tagline: They Served For Us. Now We Serve Them.
value_props: No VA overlays - we underwrite directly to VA guidelines
No minimum credit score requirements like other lenders (no 620 overlay)
In-house underwriting team specializing in VA loans
We serve those who served - veteran appreciation
Fast closings with dedicated VA loan specialists

verification_strategy.smoke_test: Visit homepage, complete quiz funnel with test data, verify lead posts to webhook, check blog posts render correctly, run Lighthouse audit

checkpoints.server: Lead webhook receives POST with all quiz data
checkpoints.client: Quiz completes without errors, thank you page shows
checkpoints.visual: Homepage displays veteran appreciation messaging, quiz is prominent
checkpoints.integration: All 20 blog posts render, SEO meta tags present

stories.title: Initialize Next.js project with Tailwind
stories.description: As a developer, I want to initialize a Next.js 16+ project with Tailwind CSS so that I have a foundation for the VA loan website.
acceptance_criteria: Given I run npm install, when dependencies install, then no errors occur
Given the project, when I run npm run dev, then the dev server starts on localhost:3000
Given Tailwind config, when I check colors, then military-inspired palette is configured (Navy #0A1628, OD Green #556B2F, Gold #D4A84B, Cream #F5F5DC)
verification_steps: Run npm install and verify success
Run npm run dev and verify server starts
stories.notes: Next.js 16.1.6 initialized with Tailwind CSS. Military color palette configured in globals.css. Dependencies: react-hook-form, zod, lucide-react, resend installed.

stories.title: Create shared UI components
stories.description: As a developer, I want reusable UI components so that the site maintains visual consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Given Button component, when rendered, then it displays with gold/navy styling and hover states
Given Header component, when rendered, then it shows logo placeholder, navigation, and CTA button
Given Footer component, when rendered, then it displays NMLS disclosure, contact info, and links
verification_steps: Check Footer for NMLS disclosure text
stories.notes: Created Button (4 variants, 3 sizes), Header (responsive with mobile menu), Footer (NMLS #173855 disclosure, contact info, navigation links).

stories.title: Build homepage hero section
stories.description: As a veteran visitor, I want to see an impactful hero section so that I immediately understand this site serves veterans like me.
acceptance_criteria: Given I visit the homepage, when it loads, then I see 'They Served For Us. Now We Serve Them.' messaging
Given the hero, when I view it, then I see a prominent CTA to start the loan quiz
Given the hero, when I view it, then I see trust indicators (VA approved, no overlays, in-house underwriting)
verification_steps: Visit homepage and verify hero messaging
Verify CTA button is prominent and links to quiz
stories.notes: Depends on Story 2. Hero should evoke respect and gratitude for veterans. Use subtle military imagery (not tacky). Include American flag colors subtly.

stories.title: Build value proposition sections
stories.description: As a veteran visitor, I want to see why Cornerstone is different so that I trust them with my VA loan.
acceptance_criteria: Given the homepage, when I scroll, then I see 'No VA Overlays' section explaining we underwrite direct to VA guidelines
Given the homepage, when I scroll, then I see 'In-House VA Experts' section about our specialized team
Given the homepage, when I scroll, then I see 'We Accept Low Credit' section explaining no 620 FICO requirement
verification_steps: Scroll homepage and verify all value prop sections exist
Verify each section clearly explains the benefit
Verify content is veteran-focused, not salesy
stories.notes: Depends on Story 3. Key differentiator: Most lenders add 620 FICO overlay - we don't. Explain what overlays are and why ours is better.

stories.title: Create VA loan quiz funnel component
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to answer questions about my loan needs so that I can get personalized assistance.
acceptance_criteria: Given I start the quiz, when I progress, then I see questions for: loan purpose, property state, credit score range, loan amount needed, property type, service branch, active/veteran status, property interest
Given each question, when I select an answer, then I auto-advance to next question with animation
Given the quiz, when I view it, then I see progress indicator showing completion percentage
verification_steps: Start quiz and verify all questions appear in sequence
stories.notes: Depends on Story 2. Quiz questions: 1) Loan purpose (purchase/refi/cash-out/IRRRL), 2) Property state, 3) Credit score (ranges including below 620), 4) Estimated loan amount, 5) Property type, 6) Military branch, 7) Service status (active/veteran/reserve/spouse), 8) Already have property in mind?

stories.title: Create quiz contact form and submission
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to submit my contact info after completing the quiz so that Cornerstone can help me.
acceptance_criteria: Given I complete quiz questions, when I reach final step, then I see contact form for name, email, phone
Given I submit valid data, when form submits, then data posts to webhook URL
Given successful submission, when I see thank you page, then I see veteran appreciation message and next steps
verification_steps: Complete quiz and verify contact form appears
Submit test data and verify webhook receives POST
Verify thank you page displays correctly
stories.notes: Depends on Story 5. Webhook URL: https://cbmtautos.duckdns.org/webhook/tanner-dscr-leads. Include honeypot spam protection and rate limiting. Thank you message: 'Thank you for your service. We'll be in touch within 24 hours.'

stories.title: Create API route for lead submission
stories.description: As a system, I want to process lead submissions so that they reach the CRM webhook.
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid POST request, when API processes it, then lead data posts to webhook
Given spam submission (honeypot filled), when API processes it, then it returns success but doesn't post
Given rate limiting threshold exceeded, when API processes it, then it returns success but doesn't post
verification_steps: POST test lead data to API route
stories.notes: Depends on Story 6. Include: honeypot field, rate limiting (5 per 15 min per IP), minimum form time check (3 seconds). Post all quiz answers plus contact info to webhook.

stories.title: Build blog infrastructure
stories.description: As a developer, I want a blog system so that we can publish SEO-optimized content.
acceptance_criteria: Given blog infrastructure, when I check data folder, then posts.json exists for blog storage
Given a blog post, when I visit /blog/[slug], then the post renders with full content
Given blog listing, when I visit /blog, then I see all posts sorted by date
verification_steps: Visit a blog post URL and verify rendering
stories.notes: Depends on Story 2. Follow same pattern as DSCR site blog. Include: ArticleSchema, BreadcrumbSchema, FAQSchema generation. Support markdown content with heading parsing.

stories.title: Create blog post component
stories.description: As a visitor, I want to read well-formatted blog posts so that I can learn about VA loans.
acceptance_criteria: Given a blog post, when I view it, then I see title, author, publish date, and formatted content
Given blog content, when I view it, then headings are properly styled with anchor links
Given a blog post, when I scroll, then I see table of contents for navigation
verification_steps: View a blog post and verify all elements render
Verify heading styles and anchor links work
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Style for readability - generous line height, proper paragraph spacing. Include TL;DR section at top for GEO optimization.

stories.title: Write blog post 1: VA Loan Eligibility Requirements
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to learn about VA loan eligibility so that I know if I qualify.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers COE requirements, service minimums, discharge status
Given the content, when I analyze it, then it's 2000+ words and doesn't sound AI-generated
Given SEO, when I check meta tags, then title, description, and schema are optimized
verification_steps: Read post and verify comprehensive coverage
Check SEO meta tags and schema
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Topics: COE (Certificate of Eligibility), service requirements by era, reserve/guard requirements, surviving spouse eligibility. Include FAQ section for schema. Use GEO optimization techniques.

stories.title: Write blog post 2: VA Loan Credit Score Requirements
stories.description: As a veteran with credit concerns, I want to understand credit requirements so that I know my options.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains VA has no minimum score but lenders add overlays
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains Cornerstone underwrites direct to VA (no 620 overlay)
Given the content, when I analyze it, then it's 2000+ words and naturally written
verification_steps: Read post and verify credit score coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Key message: VA doesn't set minimum FICO, but most lenders add 620-640 overlays. Cornerstone doesn't. This is our key differentiator. Include real examples of veterans with lower scores getting approved.

stories.title: Write blog post 3: VA Loan vs Conventional Loan Comparison
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to compare VA loans to conventional so that I can make an informed choice.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it compares: down payment, PMI, rates, closing costs, credit requirements
Given the content, when I read it, then it clearly shows VA loan advantages
Given the format, when I view it, then comparison tables are easy to scan
verification_steps: Read post and verify comprehensive comparison
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Highlight: 0% down, no PMI, competitive rates, no prepayment penalty. Include real cost savings examples. Use GEO-optimized statistics with sources.

stories.title: Write blog post 4: VA Funding Fee Explained
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to understand the VA funding fee so that I'm not surprised at closing.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains fee amounts by down payment and use (first/subsequent)
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains exemptions for disabled veterans
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains the fee can be rolled into loan
verification_steps: Read post and verify fee table accuracy
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Include 2024 funding fee table. Explain exemptions: 10%+ disability rating, Purple Heart recipients, surviving spouses. Can be financed into loan amount.

stories.title: Write blog post 5: VA IRRRL Streamline Refinance Guide
stories.description: As a veteran with existing VA loan, I want to learn about IRRRL so that I can lower my rate.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains IRRRL benefits and requirements
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers no appraisal, no income verification benefits
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains when IRRRL makes sense vs cash-out
verification_steps: Read post and verify IRRRL coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Interest Rate Reduction Refinance Loan. Benefits: no appraisal needed, no income/employment verification, can close quickly. Must have existing VA loan. Include break-even analysis example.

stories.title: Write blog post 6: VA Cash-Out Refinance Complete Guide
stories.description: As a veteran with equity, I want to learn about VA cash-out refi so that I can access my equity.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains cash-out refi process and requirements
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers up to 100% LTV (VA benefit)
Given the content, when I read it, then it compares to HELOC and other options
verification_steps: Read post and verify cash-out coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. VA allows up to 100% LTV cash-out (conventional typically 80%). Can also use to convert conventional to VA. Include use cases: debt consolidation, home improvement, investment.

stories.title: Write blog post 7: VA Loan for First-Time Homebuyers
stories.description: As a first-time veteran homebuyer, I want a comprehensive guide so that I understand the process.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers entire homebuying process step-by-step
Given the content, when I read it, then it addresses first-timer concerns and questions
Given the content, when I read it, then it includes checklist and timeline
verification_steps: Read post and verify step-by-step coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Steps: 1) Get COE, 2) Get pre-approved, 3) Find VA-savvy agent, 4) House hunt, 5) Make offer, 6) Appraisal, 7) Underwriting, 8) Close. Include timeline expectations.

stories.title: Write blog post 8: VA Loan Property Requirements
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to know what properties qualify for VA loans so that I search correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers MPRs (Minimum Property Requirements)
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains what can cause appraisal issues
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers condos, multi-family, manufactured homes
verification_steps: Read post and verify property requirement coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. MPRs: safe, sanitary, structurally sound. Common issues: peeling paint, roof damage, HVAC problems. Condo must be VA-approved. Multi-family up to 4 units allowed.

stories.title: Write blog post 9: VA Loan Closing Costs Breakdown
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to understand closing costs so that I can budget properly.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it breaks down each closing cost line item
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains which fees VA prohibits veterans from paying
Given the content, when I read it, then it provides realistic cost estimates
verification_steps: Read post and verify closing cost breakdown
Verify VA fee restrictions are accurate
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. VA prohibits certain fees: attorney fees in some states, prepayment penalties. Typical costs: funding fee, appraisal, title, recording. Seller can pay up to 4% concessions.

stories.title: Write blog post 10: VA Loan Limits and Entitlement Explained
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to understand entitlement and limits so that I know how much I can borrow.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains full vs remaining entitlement
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains 2020 Blue Water Navy Act (no limits with full entitlement)
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers having multiple VA loans
verification_steps: Read post and verify entitlement explanation
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Post-2020: No loan limits with full entitlement. Partial entitlement still has county limits. Can have multiple VA loans (one for each property). Restoration of entitlement rules.

stories.title: Write blog post 11: VA Loan for Investment Property
stories.description: As a veteran investor, I want to know if I can use VA loan for investment so that I can build wealth.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains occupancy requirements
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers multi-family house hacking strategy
Given the content, when I read it, then it addresses PCS and renting previous VA home
verification_steps: Read post and verify occupancy rules are clear
Verify house hacking strategy is explained
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. VA requires owner occupancy (60 days). House hack: buy 2-4 unit, live in one, rent others. After PCS can rent previous home and get new VA loan. Can restore entitlement after selling.

stories.title: Write blog post 12: VA Loan for Surviving Spouses
stories.description: As a surviving spouse, I want to know if I qualify for VA benefits so that I can honor my spouse's service.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers unremarried surviving spouse eligibility
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains remarried spouse rules after age 57
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers VA DIC and funding fee exemption
verification_steps: Read post and verify surviving spouse eligibility
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Unremarried surviving spouses of veterans who died in service or from service-connected disability qualify. Remarried after 57? Still eligible. Exempt from funding fee. Compassionate, respectful tone essential.

stories.title: Write blog post 13: VA Loan Appraisal Process
stories.description: As a veteran homebuyer, I want to understand the VA appraisal so that I know what to expect.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains VA appraisal vs conventional
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers NOV (Notice of Value) and Tidewater process
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains appraisal gap strategies
verification_steps: Read post and verify appraisal process coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. VA appraisers check MPRs + value. Tidewater: appraiser warns if value may come in low. NOV stays with property 6 months. Low appraisal options: renegotiate, pay gap, walk away, appeal.

stories.title: Write blog post 14: VA Loan for National Guard and Reserves
stories.description: As a Guard/Reserve member, I want to know my VA loan eligibility so that I can use my benefits.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers 6-year service requirement for Guard/Reserve
Given the content, when I read it, then it explains activation periods counting toward eligibility
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers different funding fee for reserves
verification_steps: Read post and verify Guard/Reserve requirements
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Guard/Reserve: 6 years required OR 90+ days Title 10 activation. Different funding fee schedule. Can combine active + reserve time. Include specific examples.

stories.title: Write blog post 15: How to Get Your VA Certificate of Eligibility
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to know how to get my COE so that I can start the loan process.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers all COE methods (lender portal, eBenefits, mail)
Given the content, when I read it, then it includes step-by-step instructions with screenshots concept
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers documents needed by service type
verification_steps: Read post and verify all COE methods covered
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Methods: 1) Lender pulls via VA portal (fastest), 2) eBenefits/VA.gov self-service, 3) Mail VA Form 26-1880. Documents: DD-214 (veterans), statement of service (active).

stories.title: Write blog post 16: VA Loan Myths Debunked
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to learn the truth about VA loans so that I'm not misled by myths.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it debunks 10+ common VA loan myths
Given the content, when I read it, then it provides factual corrections with sources
Given the format, when I view it, then each myth is clearly separated and debunked
verification_steps: Read post and verify 10+ myths covered
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Myths: 1) VA loans take forever, 2) Sellers won't accept VA offers, 3) You can only use VA loan once, 4) VA loans are only for first-time buyers, 5) You need perfect credit, etc.

stories.title: Write blog post 17: VA Loan After Bankruptcy or Foreclosure
stories.description: As a veteran who had financial hardship, I want to know if I can still get a VA loan.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers waiting periods after bankruptcy/foreclosure
Given the content, when I read it, then it emphasizes Cornerstone's no-overlay policy helps here
Given the content, when I read it, then it provides path forward for rebuilding
verification_steps: Read post and verify waiting period information
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Chapter 7: 2 years from discharge. Chapter 13: 1 year with court approval. Foreclosure: 2 years. VA is more forgiving than conventional. Cornerstone's direct-to-VA underwriting helps veterans in these situations.

stories.title: Write blog post 18: VA Loan vs FHA Loan Comparison
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to compare VA to FHA so that I choose the best option.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it compares down payment, PMI/MIP, rates, limits
Given the content, when I read it, then it clearly shows when VA is better (almost always)
Given the format, when I view it, then comparison table is easy to scan
verification_steps: Read post and verify comprehensive comparison
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. VA advantages: 0% down vs 3.5%, no PMI vs lifetime MIP on FHA, no loan limits, better rates typically. FHA only better if: credit below 580 or non-veteran.

stories.title: Write blog post 19: VA Loan for Disabled Veterans
stories.description: As a disabled veteran, I want to know my additional VA loan benefits.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it covers funding fee exemption for 10%+ disability
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers SADGP (adapted housing grants)
Given the content, when I read it, then it maintains respectful, empowering tone
verification_steps: Read post and verify disability benefits coverage
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Benefits: Funding fee waived (10%+ rating), SAH/SHA grants for adapting homes, Purple Heart recipients exempt. Tone: empowering, not pitying. These veterans deserve this.

stories.title: Write blog post 20: Current VA Loan Rates and Market Analysis
stories.description: As a veteran, I want to understand current VA rates so that I can time my purchase.
acceptance_criteria: Given the blog post, when I read it, then it explains how VA rates work and current market
Given the content, when I read it, then it covers rate factors (credit, down payment, loan type)
Given the content, when I read it, then it includes rate lock strategies
verification_steps: Read post and verify rate explanation
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Explain: VA rates typically 0.25-0.5% lower than conventional. Factors affecting rate. When to lock. ARM vs fixed. Include: get personalized quote CTA.

stories.title: Implement SEO meta tags and schema markup
stories.description: As a search engine, I want proper structured data so that I can index and display the site correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given any page, when I view source, then proper meta tags exist (title, description, OG)
Given homepage, when I check schema, then Organization and LocalBusiness schemas exist
Given blog posts, when I check schema, then Article, FAQ, and HowTo schemas exist where appropriate
verification_steps: Run Google Rich Results Test on homepage
Check meta tags on all page types
stories.notes: Depends on Stories 8, 9. Include: Organization schema with NMLS, LocalBusiness if applicable, Article schema for blogs, FAQ schema from blog content, BreadcrumbList for navigation.

stories.title: Run GEO content optimization on all blog posts
stories.description: As content, I want to be optimized for AI search engines so that I get cited by ChatGPT, Perplexity, etc.
acceptance_criteria: Given each blog post, when analyzed, then it includes quotable statements with statistics
Given each blog post, when analyzed, then it has TL;DR summary at top
Given each blog post, when analyzed, then it uses GEO-optimized formatting (Q&A sections, expert attribution)
verification_steps: Review each post for GEO optimization
stories.notes: Depends on Stories 10-29. Use /geo-content-optimizer skill. Add: TL;DR at top, quotable statistics, Q&A format where appropriate, expert attribution, FAQ schema content.

stories.title: Run SEO-GEO audit on entire site
stories.description: As a site, I want comprehensive SEO and GEO optimization so that I rank well in traditional and AI search.
acceptance_criteria: Given site audit, when run, then all pages have optimized meta tags
Given site audit, when run, then schema markup validates on all pages
Given site audit, when run, then content passes GEO score threshold
files_likely_affected: Multiple files based on audit results
stories.notes: Depends on Story 31. Use /seo-geo skill for comprehensive audit. Fix any issues found. Target: 90+ SEO score, strong GEO optimization indicators.

stories.title: Create sitemap.xml and robots.txt
stories.description: As a search engine, I want sitemap and robots.txt so that I can crawl the site efficiently.
acceptance_criteria: Given sitemap.xml, when accessed, then all pages are listed with proper priorities
Given robots.txt, when accessed, then it allows crawling and references sitemap
Given AI crawlers, when checking robots.txt, then they are explicitly allowed
stories.notes: Depends on Story 8. Include all blog posts in sitemap. Allow: GPTBot, Claude-Web, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended. Reference llms.txt if created.

stories.title: Add analytics tracking
stories.description: As a marketer, I want analytics so that I can measure site performance.
acceptance_criteria: Given any page, when it loads, then GA4 pageview fires
Given quiz completion, when lead submits, then conversion event fires
Given quiz steps, when user progresses, then step events fire
verification_steps: Submit test lead and verify conversion
Progress through quiz and verify step events
stories.notes: Depends on Stories 5, 6. Events: page_view, quiz_start, quiz_step_{n}, quiz_complete, lead_submit, phone_click. Include Facebook Pixel if ID provided.

stories.title: Final QA and Lighthouse audit
stories.description: As a developer, I want to ensure the site is production-ready with excellent performance.
acceptance_criteria: Given Lighthouse audit, when run on homepage, then scores are 90+ on all metrics
Given all pages, when tested, then no console errors appear
Given mobile view, when tested, then site is fully responsive
verification_steps: Run Lighthouse on homepage - verify 90+ scores
Test all pages for console errors
stories.notes: Depends on all previous stories. Check: Performance (90+), Accessibility (90+), Best Practices (90+), SEO (90+). Fix any issues found. Verify Google Ads readiness (fast load, clear CTAs).

custom_checks: Quiz funnel submits leads to webhook successfully
All 20 blog posts are 2000+ words and human-written

context_notes: VA Loan lead funnel for Cornerstone First Mortgage. KEY DIFFERENTIATOR: We underwrite directly to VA guidelines with NO overlays (no 620 FICO minimum like other lenders). In-house VA specialist underwriting team. Theme: 'They Served For Us. Now We Serve Them.' - respectful veteran appreciation. Webhook: https://cbmtautos.duckdns.org/webhook/tanner-dscr-leads. 20 blog posts required, 2000+ words each, written naturally (not AI-sounding). Use GEO optimization (/geo-content-optimizer) and SEO (/seo-geo) skills. Design: slick, modern, Google Ads ready. Project folder: C:\Users\tanne\Desktop\CLAUDE\va-loan-site
